summary: PRED-072 (TTB ±15 nT diurnal) has been silently ignored since 2026-04-11 (9 days past window closure) but has no entry in data/uncounted-failures.json. Methodological inconsistency with FAIL-009 (PRED-105 added at 52h). Decider should allocate FAIL-010 and add a parallel silent-ignore entry.
detail: In today's poll record chg-20260420-0414-001 the poller explicitly flagged this gap: 'PRED-072 ("This week" window from 2026-04-04) also still PENDING — PROSPECTIVE with no dome-side movement. ... Also watch PRED-072.' PRED-072 was registered 2026-04-04 (git commit 54476366, 2026-04-05 — data predated registration per our predictions.json notes), claims '±15 nT TTB H-component diurnal fluctuation at local midnight', and nominates a 7-night test window 2026-04-04 through 2026-04-11. Today is 2026-04-20 — 9 days past closure. The dome site still shows PRED-072 as PENDING — PROSPECTIVE with no PASS/FAIL/REFINED label; our predictions.json already has window_expired=true, window_expired_at='2026-04-11', dome_silent_on_expiry=true. Adding FAIL-009 for PRED-105 at 52h while leaving PRED-072 at 216h untracked is an internal-consistency gap. Counter-consideration: PRED-072's our_verdict is 'standard_physics' (standard ionospheric Sq variation), so arguably it is not a falsified claim in the scientific-verdict sense. But the uncounted-failures scorecard tracks the bookkeeping fact that the dome announced a prospective prediction and silently let the window close — which is orthogonal to our scientific assessment. PRED-105 is also bioelectromagnetics (our verdict pending / methodology weak), yet it was still added. The same bookkeeping standard should apply to PRED-072.

recommended_fail_entry.what_actually_happened: Silent ignore: prediction window expired with no outcome reported.
recommended_fail_entry.outcome: Window closed 2026-04-11. Dome site still shows PENDING-PROSPECTIVE 216+ hours (9 days) later with no PASS/FAIL/REFINED label. Silent-ignore pattern: no verdict issued, no denominator change. This is a longer silent-ignore interval than FAIL-009/PRED-105.
recommended_fail_entry.prediction: PRED-072: ±15 nT TTB (Tsumeb) short-term H-component fluctuation at local midnight, claimed driven by 'toroidal aetheric return current cycling through the sub-terrestrial Sump'. 7-night test window April 4-11, 2026. Registered 2026-04-04 with SHA-256 anchor (c7274f8fd69d3109045726c1e59c2e96e6051f3c8549428bccc075ce6b1a6b48); git commit was 2026-04-05 and INTERMAGNET TTB data was publicly available before registration (data_predates_registration=true).
recommended_fail_entry.prediction_name: PRED-072 TTB ±15 nT diurnal (aetheric return current)
recommended_fail_entry.summary: PRED-072 TTB diurnal (7-night window) closed 2026-04-11, dome silent 9 days later

priority_note: MEDIUM. Not as urgent as the FAIL-009 text patch (which ships wrong content today). PRED-072 is a silence-of-omission rather than active misinformation. But the longer we leave the asymmetry (PRED-105 tracked at 52h, PRED-072 untracked at 216h), the more likely the curmudgeon or an external reviewer spots the inconsistency.
counter_considerations: PRED-072 our_verdict='standard_physics' means we already argue the predicted effect is just Sq variation. A reviewer could argue: we shouldn't track 'dome silently dropped a prediction we think was never dome-specific anyway'. Counter: uncounted-failures tracks the dome's methodological behaviour, not our scientific verdict on the underlying physics.

PRED-072 restates WIN-042 territory (both about TTB geomagnetic behaviour under 'aetheric' framing). Adding a FAIL entry could be seen as double-counting WIN-042's verdict. Counter: FAIL is about a prospective-prediction silent-drop event, distinct from the retrospective WIN. Both can coexist.

Adding FAIL-010 at 9 days post-window is very late; one could argue the statute-of-limitations has passed and a silent-drop added now would look retrofitted. Counter: the 48–72h threshold was a lower bound, not an upper bound; 216h just crossed it more decisively.
